revision.comment: larger scope
revision.text:
{{for|more comprehensive information and a larger scope|Wikipedia:Disambiguation}} {{pp-protected|small=yes}} {{Information page|H:DIS|HELP:DAB}} {{Reader help}} {{Dabnav}} '''Disambiguation''' pages on Wikipedia are used as a process of resolving conflicts in [[Wikipedia:Article titles|article titles]] that occur when a single term can be associated with more than one topic, making that term likely to be the natural title for more than one article. In other words, disambiguations are paths leading to different articles which could, in principle, have the same title.
For example, the word "Mercury" can refer to several things, including an element, a planet, and a Roman god. Since only one Wikipedia page can have the generic name [[Mercury]], ''unambiguous'' article titles are used for each of these topics: [[Mercury (element)]], [[Mercury (planet)]], [[Mercury (mythology)]], etc. There must then be a way to direct the reader to the correct specific article when the ambiguous word "Mercury" is referenced by linking, browsing or searching; this is what is known as ''disambiguation''. In this case, it is achieved using Mercury as the title of a ''disambiguation page''.
There are three common disambiguation scenarios, of which the following are examples: * The page at [[Joker]] is a ''[[disambiguation page]]'', leading to all the alternative uses of "Joker". It has a {{tl|Disambiguation}} template at the bottom. * The page at [[Rice]] is about one usage, called the ''[[Wikipedia:Primary topic|primary topic]]'' or ''[[Wikipedia:Broad-concept article|broad-concept]]'', and there is a short message at the top of the article, called a ''[[Wikipedia:Hatnote|hatnote]]'' and created with at [[Wikipedia:Hatnote#Hatnote templates|Hatnote templates]], guiding readers to [[Rice (disambiguation)|Rice (disambiguation''')''']] for the other less common uses of the term. * The page at [[Anita Hill]] is about the primary topic, and there are only two other uses. The other uses are linked directly using the hatnotes created with the {{tl|For}} template; no disambiguation page is needed.
Occasionally, a link may direct a reader to a disambiguation page instead of the intended article. The original page can be edited to link directly to the correct destination. Such edits are encouraged, as [[Wikipedia:What is Wikipedia|Wikipedia is a collaborative platform]] that welcomes contributions from all users.
Occasionally, a reader may follow a link that ends up at a disambiguation page rather than at one of the links shown on that page. The original page can be edited to link directly to its intended destination. Feel free to make such edits; everyone is welcome to improve Wikipedia, including you. It could be you who makes the overall experience of Wikipedia that much better.
== See also ==
* [[Help:Contents]] * [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Disambiguation pages]] * [[Wikipedia:Template messages for disambiguation and redirection]] * [[Wikipedia:Disambiguation detector]] * the {{tl|Distinguish}} template in [[Wikipedia:Hatnote]]
{{-}} ---- <inputbox> type=search width=30 namespaces=Main**,Portal**,Category**,Help**, searchbuttonlabel=Search break=no </inputbox> ----
[[Category:Reader help]] [[Category:Wikipedia disambiguation| ]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: importing wikipedia
revision.text:
{{for|more comprehensive information and a larger scope|Wikipedia:Disambiguation}} {{pp-protected|small=yes}} {{Information page|H:DIS|HELP:DAB}} {{Reader help}} {{Dabnav}} '''Disambiguation''' pages on Wikipedia are used as a process of resolving conflicts in [[Wikipedia:Article titles|article titles]] that occur when a single term can be associated with more than one topic, making that term likely to be the natural title for more than one article. In other words, disambiguations are paths leading to different articles which could, in principle, have the same title.
For example, the word "Mercury" can refer to several things, including an element, a planet, and a Roman god. Since only one Wikipedia page can have the generic name [[Mercury]], ''unambiguous'' article titles are used for each of these topics: [[Mercury (element)]], [[Mercury (planet)]], [[Mercury (mythology)]], etc. There must then be a way to direct the reader to the correct specific article when the ambiguous word "Mercury" is referenced by linking, browsing or searching; this is what is known as ''disambiguation''. In this case, it is achieved using Mercury as the title of a ''disambiguation page''.
There are three common disambiguation scenarios, of which the following are examples: * The page at [[Joker]] is a ''[[disambiguation page]]'', leading to all the alternative uses of "Joker". It has a {{tl|Disambiguation}} template at the bottom. * The page at [[Rice]] is about one usage, called the ''[[Wikipedia:Primary topic|primary topic]]'' or ''[[Wikipedia:Broad-concept article|broad-concept]]'', and there is a short message at the top of the article, called a ''[[Wikipedia:Hatnote|hatnote]]'' and created with at [[Wikipedia:Hatnote#Hatnote templates|Hatnote templates]], guiding readers to [[Rice (disambiguation)|Rice (disambiguation''')''']] for the other less common uses of the term. * The page at [[Anita Hill]] is about the primary topic, and there are only two other uses. The other uses are linked directly using the hatnotes created with the {{tl|For}} template; no disambiguation page is needed.
Occasionally, a link may direct a reader to a disambiguation page instead of the intended article. The original page can be edited to link directly to the correct destination. Such edits are encouraged, as [[Wikipedia:What is Wikipedia|Wikipedia is a collaborative platform]] that welcomes contributions from all users.
Occasionally, a reader may follow a link that ends up at a disambiguation page rather than at one of the links shown on that page. The original page can be edited to link directly to its intended destination. Feel free to make such edits; everyone is welcome to improve Wikipedia, including you. It could be you who makes the overall experience of Wikipedia that much better.
== See also ==

{{for|more comprehensive information and a larger scope|Wikipedia:Disambiguation}} {{pp-protected|small=yes}} {{Information page|H:DIS|HELP:DAB}} {{Reader help}} {{Dabnav}} '''Disambiguation''' pages on Wikipedia are used as a process of resolving conflicts in [[Wikipedia:Article titles|article titles]] that occur when a single term can be associated with more than one topic, making that term likely to be the natural title for more than one article. In other words, disambiguations are paths leading to different articles which could, in principle, have the same title.
For example, the word "Mercury" can refer to several things, including an element, a planet, and a Roman god. Since only one Wikipedia page can have the generic name [[Mercury]], ''unambiguous'' article titles are used for each of these topics: [[Mercury (element)]], [[Mercury (planet)]], [[Mercury (mythology)]], etc. There must then be a way to direct the reader to the correct specific article when the ambiguous word "Mercury" is referenced by linking, browsing or searching; this is what is known as ''disambiguation''. In this case, it is achieved using Mercury as the title of a ''disambiguation page''.
There are three common disambiguation scenarios, of which the following are examples: * The page at [[Joker]] is a ''[[disambiguation page]]'', leading to all the alternative uses of "Joker". It has a {{tl|Disambiguation}} template at the bottom. * The page at [[Rice]] is about one usage, called the ''[[Wikipedia:Primary topic|primary topic]]'' or ''[[Wikipedia:Broad-concept article|broad-concept]]'', and there is a short message at the top of the article, called a ''[[Wikipedia:Hatnote|hatnote]]'' and created with at [[Wikipedia:Hatnote#Hatnote templates|Hatnote templates]], guiding readers to [[Rice (disambiguation)|Rice (disambiguation''')''']] for the other less common uses of the term. * The page at [[Anita Hill]] is about the primary topic, and there are only two other uses. The other uses are linked directly using the hatnotes created with the {{tl|For}} template; no disambiguation page is needed.
Occasionally, a link may direct a reader to a disambiguation page instead of the intended article. The original page can be edited to link directly to the correct destination. Occasionally, a reader may follow a link that ends up at a disambiguation page rather than at one of the links shown on that page. revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:List of Shikinaisha]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{for|more comprehensive information and a larger scope|Wikipedia:Disambiguation}} {{pp-protected|small=yes}} {{Information page|H:DIS|HELP:DAB}} {{Reader help}} {{Dabnav}} '''Disambiguation''' pages on Wikipedia are used as a process of resolving conflicts in [[Wikipedia:Article titles|article titles]] that occur when a single term can be associated with more than one topic, making that term likely to be the natural title for more than one article. In other words, disambiguations are paths leading to different articles which could, in principle, have the same title.
For example, the word "Mercury" can refer to several things, including an element, a planet, and a Roman god. Since only one Wikipedia page can have the generic name [[Mercury]], ''unambiguous'' article titles are used for each of these topics: [[Mercury (element)]], [[Mercury (planet)]], [[Mercury (mythology)]], etc. There must then be a way to direct the reader to the correct specific article when the ambiguous word "Mercury" is referenced by linking, browsing or searching; this is what is known as ''disambiguation''. In this case, it is achieved using Mercury as the title of a ''disambiguation page''.
There are three common disambiguation scenarios, of which the following are examples: * The page at [[Joker]] is a ''[[disambiguation page]]'', leading to all the alternative uses of "Joker". It has a {{tl|Disambiguation}} template at the bottom. * The page at [[Rice]] is about one usage, called the ''[[Wikipedia:Primary topic|primary topic]]'' or ''[[Wikipedia:Broad-concept article|broad-concept]]'', and there is a short message at the top of the article, called a ''[[Wikipedia:Hatnote|hatnote]]'' and created with at [[Wikipedia:Hatnote#Hatnote templates|Hatnote templates]], guiding readers to [[Rice (disambiguation)|Rice (disambiguation''')''']] for the other less common uses of the term. * The page at [[Anita Hill]] is about the primary topic, and there are only two other uses. The other uses are linked directly using the hatnotes created with the {{tl|For}} template; no disambiguation page is needed.
Occasionally, a link may direct a reader to a disambiguation page instead of the intended article. The original page can be edited to link directly to the correct destination. Such edits are encouraged, as [[Wikipedia:What is Wikipedia|Wikipedia is a collaborative platform]] that welcomes contributions from all users.
Occasionally, a reader may follow a link that ends up at a disambiguation page rather than at one of the links shown on that page. The original page can be edited to link directly to its intended destination. Feel free to make such edits; everyone is welcome to improve Wikipedia, including you. It could be you who makes the overall experience of Wikipedia that much better.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{for|more comprehensive information and a larger scope|Wikipedia:Disambiguation}} {{pp-protected|small=yes}} {{Information page|H:DIS|HELP:DAB}} {{Reader help}} {{Dabnav}} '''Disambiguation''' pages on Wikipedia are used as a process of resolving conflicts in [[Wikipedia:Article titles|article titles]] that occur when a single term can be associated with more than one topic, making that term likely to be the natural title for more than one article. In other words, disambiguations are paths leading to different articles which could, in principle, have the same title.
For example, the word "Mercury" can refer to several things, including an element, a planet, and a Roman god. Since only one Wikipedia page can have the generic name [[Mercury]], ''unambiguous'' article titles are used for each of these topics: [[Mercury (element)]], [[Mercury (planet)]], [[Mercury (mythology)]], etc. There must then be a way to direct the reader to the correct specific article when the ambiguous word "Mercury" is referenced by linking, browsing or searching; this is what is known as ''disambiguation''. In this case, it is achieved using Mercury as the title of a ''disambiguation page''.
There are three common disambiguation scenarios, of which the following are examples: * The page at [[Joker]] is a ''[[disambiguation page]]'', leading to all the alternative uses of "Joker". It has a {{tl|Disambiguation}} template at the bottom. * The page at [[Rice]] is about one usage, called the ''[[Wikipedia:Primary topic|primary topic]]'' or ''[[Wikipedia:Broad-concept article|broad-concept]]'', and there is a short message at the top of the article, called a ''[[Wikipedia:Hatnote|hatnote]]'' and created with at [[Wikipedia:Hatnote#Hatnote templates|Hatnote templates]], guiding readers to [[Rice (disambiguation)|Rice (disambiguation''')''']] for the other less common uses of the term. * The page at [[Anita Hill]] is about the primary topic, and there are only two other uses. The other uses are linked directly using the hatnotes created with the {{tl|For}} template; no disambiguation page is needed.
Occasionally, a link may direct a reader to a disambiguation page instead of the intended article. The original page can be edited to link directly to the correct destination. Such edits are encouraged, as [[Wikipedia:What is Wikipedia|Wikipedia is a collaborative platform]] that welcomes contributions from all users.
Occasionally, a reader may follow a link that ends up at a disambiguation page rather than at one of the links shown on that page. The original page can be edited to link directly to its intended destination. Feel free to make such edits; everyone is welcome to improve Wikipedia, including you. It could be you who makes the overall experience of Wikipedia that much better.

{{for|more comprehensive information and a larger scope|Wikipedia:Disambiguation}} {{pp-protected|small=yes}} {{Information page|H:DIS|HELP:DAB}} {{Reader help}} {{Dabnav}} '''Disambiguation''' pages on Wikipedia are used as a process of resolving conflicts in [[Wikipedia:Article titles|article titles]] that occur when a single term can be associated with more than one topic, making that term likely to be the natural title for more than one article. In other words, disambiguations are paths leading to different articles which could, in principle, have the same title.
For example, the word "Mercury" can refer to several things, including an element, a planet, and a Roman god. Since only one Wikipedia page can have the generic name [[Mercury]], ''unambiguous'' article titles are used for each of these topics: [[Mercury (element)]], [[Mercury (planet)]], [[Mercury (mythology)]], etc. There must then be a way to direct the reader to the correct specific article when the ambiguous word "Mercury" is referenced by linking, browsing or searching; this is what is known as ''disambiguation''. In this case, it is achieved using Mercury as the title of a ''disambiguation page''.
There are three common disambiguation scenarios, of which the following are examples: * The page at [[Joker]] is a ''[[disambiguation page]]'', leading to all the alternative uses of "Joker". It has a {{tl|Disambiguation}} template at the bottom. * The page at [[Rice]] is about one usage, called the ''[[Wikipedia:Primary topic|primary topic]]'' or ''[[Wikipedia:Broad-concept article|broad-concept]]'', and there is a short message at the top of the article, called a ''[[Wikipedia:Hatnote|hatnote]]'' and created with at [[Wikipedia:Hatnote#Hatnote templates|Hatnote templates]], guiding readers to [[Rice (disambiguation)|Rice (disambiguation''')''']] for the other less common uses of the term. * The page at [[Anita Hill]] is about the primary topic, and there are only two other uses. The other uses are linked directly using the hatnotes created with the {{tl|For}} template; no disambiguation page is needed.
Occasionally, a link may direct a reader to a disambiguation page instead of the intended article. The original page can be edited to link directly to the correct destination. Such edits are encouraged, as [[Wikipedia:What is Wikipedia|Wikipedia is a collaborative platform]] that welcomes contributions from all users.
Occasionally, a reader may follow a link that ends up at a disambiguation page rather than at one of the links shown on that page. The original page can be edited to link directly to its intended destination. Feel free to make such edits; everyone is welcome to improve Wikipedia, including you. It could be you who makes the overall experience of Wikipedia that much better.
== See also ==

{{for|more comprehensive information and a larger scope|Wikipedia:Disambiguation}} {{pp-protected|small=yes}} {{Information page|H:DIS|HELP:DAB}} {{Reader help}} {{Dabnav}} '''Disambiguation''' pages on Wikipedia are used as a process of resolving conflicts in [[Wikipedia:Article titles|article titles]] that occur when a single term can be associated with more than one topic, making that term likely to be the natural title for more than one article. In other words, disambiguations are paths leading to different articles which could, in principle, have the same title.
For example, the word "Mercury" can refer to several things, including an element, a planet, and a Roman god. Since only one Wikipedia page can have the generic name [[Mercury]], ''unambiguous'' article titles are used for each of these topics: [[Mercury (element)]], [[Mercury (planet)]], [[Mercury (mythology)]], etc. There must then be a way to direct the reader to the correct specific article when the ambiguous word "Mercury" is referenced by linking, browsing or searching; this is what is known as ''disambiguation''. In this case, it is achieved using Mercury as the title of a ''disambiguation page''.
There are three common disambiguation scenarios, of which the following are examples: * The page at [[Joker]] is a ''[[disambiguation page]]'', leading to all the alternative uses of "Joker". It has a {{tl|Disambiguation}} template at the bottom. * The page at [[Rice]] is about one usage, called the ''[[Wikipedia:Primary topic|primary topic]]'' or ''[[Wikipedia:Broad-concept article|broad-concept]]'', and there is a short message at the top of the article, called a ''[[Wikipedia:Hatnote|hatnote]]'' and created with at [[Wikipedia:Hatnote#Hatnote templates|Hatnote templates]], guiding readers to [[Rice (disambiguation)|Rice (disambiguation''')''']] for the other less common uses of the term. * The page at [[Anita Hill]] is about the primary topic, and there are only two other uses. The other uses are linked directly using the hatnotes created with the {{tl|For}} template; no disambiguation page is needed.
Occasionally, a link may direct a reader to a disambiguation page instead of the intended article. The original page can be edited to link directly to the correct destination. Such edits are encouraged, as [[Wikipedia:What is Wikipedia|Wikipedia is a collaborative platform]] that welcomes contributions from all users.
Occasionally, a reader may follow a link that ends up at a disambiguation page rather than at one of the links shown on that page. The original page can be edited to link directly to its intended destination. Feel free to make such edits; everyone is welcome to improve Wikipedia, including you. It could be you who makes the overall experience of Wikipedia that much better.

revision.text:
{{for|more comprehensive information and a larger scope|Wikipedia:Disambiguation}} {{pp-protected|small=yes}} {{Information page|H:DIS|HELP:DAB}} {{Reader help}} {{Dabnav}} '''Disambiguation''' pages on Wikipedia are used as a process of resolving conflicts in [[Wikipedia:Article titles|article titles]] that occur when a single term can be associated with more than one topic, making that term likely to be the natural title for more than one article. In other words, disambiguations are paths leading to different articles which could, in principle, have the same title.
For example, the word "Mercury" can refer to several things, including an element, a planet, and a Roman god. Since only one Wikipedia page can have the generic name [[Mercury]], ''unambiguous'' article titles are used for each of these topics: [[Mercury (element)]], [[Mercury (planet)]], [[Mercury (mythology)]], etc. There must then be a way to direct the reader to the correct specific article when the ambiguous word "Mercury" is referenced by linking, browsing or searching; this is what is known as ''disambiguation''. In this case, it is achieved using Mercury as the title of a ''disambiguation page''.
There are three common disambiguation scenarios, of which the following are examples: * The page at [[Joker]] is a ''[[disambiguation page]]'', leading to all the alternative uses of "Joker". It has a {{tl|Disambiguation}} template at the bottom. * The page at [[Rice]] is about one usage, called the ''[[Wikipedia:Primary topic|primary topic]]'' or ''[[Wikipedia:Broad-concept article|broad-concept]]'', and there is a short message at the top of the article, called a ''[[Wikipedia:Hatnote|hatnote]]'' and created with at [[Wikipedia:Hatnote#Hatnote templates|Hatnote templates]], guiding readers to [[Rice (disambiguation)|Rice (disambiguation''')''']] for the other less common uses of the term. * The page at [[Anita Hill]] is about the primary topic, and there are only two other uses. The other uses are linked directly using the hatnotes created with the {{tl|For}} template; no disambiguation page is needed.
Occasionally, a link may direct a reader to a disambiguation page instead of the intended article. The original page can be edited to link directly to the correct destination. Such edits are encouraged, as [[Wikipedia:What is Wikipedia|Wikipedia is a collaborative platform]] that welcomes contributions from all users.
Occasionally, a reader may follow a link that ends up at a disambiguation page rather than at one of the links shown on that page. The original page can be edited to link directly to its intended destination. Feel free to make such edits; everyone is welcome to improve Wikipedia, including you. revision.text:
{{for|more comprehensive information and a larger scope|Wikipedia:Disambiguation}} {{pp-protected|small=yes}} {{Information page|H:DIS|HELP:DAB}} {{Reader help}} {{Dabnav}} '''Disambiguation''' pages on Wikipedia are used as a process of resolving conflicts in [[Wikipedia:Article titles|article titles]] that occur when a single term can be associated with more than one topic, making that term likely to be the natural title for more than one article. In other words, disambiguations are paths leading to different articles which could, in principle, have the same title.
For example, the word "Mercury" can refer to several things, including an element, a planet, and a Roman god. Since only one Wikipedia page can have the generic name [[Mercury]], ''unambiguous'' article titles are used for each of these topics: [[Mercury (element)]], [[Mercury (planet)]], [[Mercury (mythology)]], etc. There must then be a way to direct the reader to the correct specific article when the ambiguous word "Mercury" is referenced by linking, browsing or searching; this is what is known as ''disambiguation''. In this case, it is achieved using Mercury as the title of a ''disambiguation page''.
There are three common disambiguation scenarios, of which the following are examples: * The page at [[Joker]] is a ''[[disambiguation page]]'', leading to all the alternative uses of "Joker". It has a {{tl|Disambiguation}} template at the bottom. * The page at [[Rice]] is about one usage, called the ''[[Wikipedia:Primary topic|primary topic]]'' or ''[[Wikipedia:Broad-concept article|broad-concept]]'', and there is a short message at the top of the article, called a ''[[Wikipedia:Hatnote|hatnote]]'' and created with at [[Wikipedia:Hatnote#Hatnote templates|Hatnote templates]], guiding readers to [[Rice (disambiguation)|Rice (disambiguation''')''']] for the other less common uses of the term. * The page at [[Anita Hill]] is about the primary topic, and there are only two other uses. The other uses are linked directly using the hatnotes created with the {{tl|For}} template; no disambiguation page is needed.
Occasionally, a link may direct a reader to a disambiguation page instead of the intended article. The original page can be edited to link directly to the correct destination. Such edits are encouraged, as [[Wikipedia:What is Wikipedia|Wikipedia is a collaborative platform]] that welcomes contributions from all users.
Occasionally, a reader may follow a link that ends up at a disambiguation page rather than at one of the links shown on that page. The original page can be edited to link directly to its intended destination. Feel free to make such edits; everyone is welcome to improve Wikipedia, including you. It could be you who makes the overall experience of Wikipedia that much better.

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{for|more comprehensive information and a larger scope|Wikipedia:Disambiguation}} {{pp-protected|small=yes}} {{Information page|H:DIS|HELP:DAB}} {{Reader help}} {{Dabnav}} '''Disambiguation''' pages on Wikipedia are used as a process of resolving conflicts in [[Wikipedia:Article titles|article titles]] that occur when a single term can be associated with more than one topic, making that term likely to be the natural title for more than one article. In other words, disambiguations are paths leading to different articles which could, in principle, have the same title.
For example, the word "Mercury" can refer to several things, including an element, a planet, and a Roman god. Since only one Wikipedia page can have the generic name [[Mercury]], ''unambiguous'' article titles are used for each of these topics: [[Mercury (element)]], [[Mercury (planet)]], [[Mercury (mythology)]], etc. There must then be a way to direct the reader to the correct specific article when the ambiguous word "Mercury" is referenced by linking, browsing or searching; this is what is known as ''disambiguation''. In this case, it is achieved using Mercury as the title of a ''disambiguation page''.
There are three common disambiguation scenarios, of which the following are examples: * The page at [[Joker]] is a ''[[disambiguation page]]'', leading to all the alternative uses of "Joker". It has a {{tl|Disambiguation}} template at the bottom. * The page at [[Rice]] is about one usage, called the ''[[Wikipedia:Primary topic|primary topic]]'' or ''[[Wikipedia:Broad-concept article|broad-concept]]'', and there is a short message at the top of the article, called a ''[[Wikipedia:Hatnote|hatnote]]'' and created with at [[Wikipedia:Hatnote#Hatnote templates|Hatnote templates]], guiding readers to [[Rice (disambiguation)|Rice (disambiguation''')''']] for the other less common uses of the term. * The page at [[:en:Anita Hill|Anita Hill]] is about the primary topic, and there are only two other uses. The other uses are linked directly using the hatnotes created with the {{tl|For}} template; no disambiguation page is needed.
Occasionally, a link may direct a reader to a disambiguation page instead of the intended article. The original page can be edited to link directly to the correct destination. Such edits are encouraged, as [[Wikipedia:What is Wikipedia|Wikipedia is a collaborative platform]] that welcomes contributions from all users.
Occasionally, a reader may follow a link that ends up at a disambiguation page rather than at one of the links shown on that page. The original page can be edited to link directly to its intended destination. Feel free to make such edits; everyone is welcome to improve Wikipedia, including you. It could be you who makes the overall experience of Wikipedia that much better.
== See also ==
* [[Help:Contents]] * [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Disambiguation pages]] * [[Wikipedia:Template messages for disambiguation and redirection]] * [[Wikipedia:Disambiguation detector]] * the {{tl|Distinguish}} template in [[Wikipedia:Hatnote]]
{{-}} ---- <inputbox> type=search width=30 namespaces=Main**,Portal**,Category**,Help**, searchbuttonlabel=Search break=no </inputbox> ----
[[Category:Reader help]] [[Category:Wikipedia disambiguation| ]] [[sn:Disambiguation]] [[sn:Disambiguation]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{for|more comprehensive information and a larger scope|Wikipedia:Disambiguation}} {{pp-protected|small=yes}} {{Information page|H:DIS|HELP:DAB}} {{Reader help}} {{Dabnav}} '''Disambiguation''' pages on Wikipedia are used as a process of resolving conflicts in [[Wikipedia:Article titles|article titles]] that occur when a single term can be associated with more than one topic, making that term likely to be the natural title for more than one article. In other words, disambiguations are paths leading to different articles which could, in principle, have the same title.
For example, the word "Mercury" can refer to several things, including an element, a planet, and a Roman god. Since only one Wikipedia page can have the generic name [[Mercury]], ''unambiguous'' article titles are used for each of these topics: [[Mercury (element)]], [[Mercury (planet)]], [[Mercury (mythology)]], etc. There must then be a way to direct the reader to the correct specific article when the ambiguous word "Mercury" is referenced by linking, browsing or searching; this is what is known as ''disambiguation''. In this case, it is achieved using Mercury as the title of a ''disambiguation page''.
There are three common disambiguation scenarios, of which the following are examples: * The page at [[Joker]] is a ''[[:en:Disambiguation page|Disambiguation page]]'', leading to all the alternative uses of "Joker". It has a {{tl|Disambiguation}} template at the bottom. * The page at [[Rice]] is about one usage, called the ''[[Wikipedia:Primary topic|primary topic]]'' or ''[[Wikipedia:Broad-concept article|broad-concept]]'', and there is a short message at the top of the article, called a ''[[Wikipedia:Hatnote|hatnote]]'' and created with at [[Wikipedia:Hatnote#Hatnote templates|Hatnote templates]], guiding readers to [[Rice (disambiguation)|Rice (disambiguation''')''']] for the other less common uses of the term. * The page at [[:en:Anita Hill|Anita Hill]] is about the primary topic, and there are only two other uses. The other uses are linked directly using the hatnotes created with the {{tl|For}} template; no disambiguation page is needed.
Occasionally, a link may direct a reader to a disambiguation page instead of the intended article. The original page can be edited to link directly to the correct destination. Such edits are encouraged, as [[Wikipedia:What is Wikipedia|Wikipedia is a collaborative platform]] that welcomes contributions from all users.
Occasionally, a reader may follow a link that ends up at a disambiguation page rather than at one of the links shown on that page. The original page can be edited to link directly to its intended destination. Feel free to make such edits; everyone is welcome to improve Wikipedia, including you. It could be you who makes the overall experience of Wikipedia that much better.
== See also ==
* [[Help:Contents]] * [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Disambiguation pages]] * [[Wikipedia:Template messages for disambiguation and redirection]] * [[Wikipedia:Disambiguation detector]] * the {{tl|Distinguish}} template in [[Wikipedia:Hatnote]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{for|more comprehensive information and a larger scope|Wikipedia:Disambiguation}} {{pp-protected|small=yes}} {{Information page|H:DIS|HELP:DAB}} {{Reader help}} {{Dabnav}} '''Disambiguation''' pages on Wikipedia are used as a process of resolving conflicts in [[Wikipedia:Article titles|article titles]] that occur when a single term can be associated with more than one topic, making that term likely to be the natural title for more than one article. In other words, disambiguations are paths leading to different articles which could, in principle, have the same title.
For example, the word "Mercury" can refer to several things, including an element, a planet, and a Roman god. Since only one Wikipedia page can have the generic name [[Mercury]], ''unambiguous'' article titles are used for each of these topics: [[Mercury (element)]], [[Mercury (planet)]], [[Mercury (mythology)]], etc. There must then be a way to direct the reader to the correct specific article when the ambiguous word "Mercury" is referenced by linking, browsing or searching; this is what is known as ''disambiguation''. In this case, it is achieved using Mercury as the title of a ''disambiguation page''.
There are three common disambiguation scenarios, of which the following are examples: * The page at [[:en:Joker|Joker]] is a ''[[:en:Disambiguation page|Disambiguation page]]'', leading to all the alternative uses of "Joker". It has a {{tl|Disambiguation}} template at the bottom. * The page at [[Rice]] is about one usage, called the ''[[Wikipedia:Primary topic|primary topic]]'' or ''[[Wikipedia:Broad-concept article|broad-concept]]'', and there is a short message at the top of the article, called a ''[[Wikipedia:Hatnote|hatnote]]'' and created with at [[Wikipedia:Hatnote#Hatnote templates|Hatnote templates]], guiding readers to [[Rice (disambiguation)|Rice (disambiguation''')''']] for the other less common uses of the term. * The page at [[:en:Anita Hill|Anita Hill]] is about the primary topic, and there are only two other uses. The other uses are linked directly using the hatnotes created with the {{tl|For}} template; no disambiguation page is needed.
Occasionally, a link may direct a reader to a disambiguation page instead of the intended article. The original page can be edited to link directly to the correct destination. Such edits are encouraged, as [[Wikipedia:What is Wikipedia|Wikipedia is a collaborative platform]] that welcomes contributions from all users.
Occasionally, a reader may follow a link that ends up at a disambiguation page rather than at one of the links shown on that page. The original page can be edited to link directly to its intended destination. Feel free to make such edits; everyone is welcome to improve Wikipedia, including you. It could be you who makes the overall experience of Wikipedia that much better.

revision.text:
{{for|more comprehensive information and a larger scope|Wikipedia:Disambiguation}} {{pp-protected|small=yes}} {{Information page|H:DIS|HELP:DAB}} {{Reader help}} {{Dabnav}} '''Disambiguation''' pages on Wikipedia are used as a process of resolving conflicts in [[Wikipedia:Article titles|article titles]] that occur when a single term can be associated with more than one topic, making that term likely to be the natural title for more than one article. In other words, disambiguations are paths leading to different articles which could, in principle, have the same title.
For example, the word "Mercury" can refer to several things, including an element, a planet, and a Roman god. Since only one Wikipedia page can have the generic name [[:en:Mercury|Mercury]], ''unambiguous'' article titles are used for each of these topics: [[Mercury (element)]], [[Mercury (planet)]], [[Mercury (mythology)]], etc. There must then be a way to direct the reader to the correct specific article when the ambiguous word "Mercury" is referenced by linking, browsing or searching; this is what is known as ''disambiguation''. In this case, it is achieved using Mercury as the title of a ''disambiguation page''.
There are three common disambiguation scenarios, of which the following are examples: * The page at [[:en:Joker|Joker]] is a ''[[:en:Disambiguation page|Disambiguation page]]'', leading to all the alternative uses of "Joker". It has a {{tl|Disambiguation}} template at the bottom. * The page at [[Rice]] is about one usage, called the ''[[Wikipedia:Primary topic|primary topic]]'' or ''[[Wikipedia:Broad-concept article|broad-concept]]'', and there is a short message at the top of the article, called a ''[[Wikipedia:Hatnote|hatnote]]'' and created with at [[Wikipedia:Hatnote#Hatnote templates|Hatnote templates]], guiding readers to [[Rice (disambiguation)|Rice (disambiguation''')''']] for the other less common uses of the term. * The page at [[:en:Anita Hill|Anita Hill]] is about the primary topic, and there are only two other uses. The other uses are linked directly using the hatnotes created with the {{tl|For}} template; no disambiguation page is needed.
Occasionally, a link may direct a reader to a disambiguation page instead of the intended article. The original page can be edited to link directly to the correct destination. Such edits are encouraged, as [[Wikipedia:What is Wikipedia|Wikipedia is a collaborative platform]] that welcomes contributions from all users.
Occasionally, a reader may follow a link that ends up at a disambiguation page rather than at one of the links shown on that page. The original page can be edited to link directly to its intended destination. Feel free to make such edits; everyone is welcome to improve Wikipedia, including you. It could be you who makes the overall experience of Wikipedia that much better.

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{for|more comprehensive information and a larger scope|Wikipedia:Disambiguation}} {{pp-protected|small=yes}} {{Information page|H:DIS|HELP:DAB}} {{Reader help}} {{Dabnav}} '''Disambiguation''' pages on Wikipedia are used as a process of resolving conflicts in [[Wikipedia:Article titles|article titles]] that occur when a single term can be associated with more than one topic, making that term likely to be the natural title for more than one article. In other words, disambiguations are paths leading to different articles which could, in principle, have the same title.
For example, the word "Mercury" can refer to several things, including an element, a planet, and a Roman god. Since only one Wikipedia page can have the generic name [[:en:Mercury|Mercury]], ''unambiguous'' article titles are used for each of these topics: [[:en:Mercury (element)|Mercury (element)]], [[Mercury (planet)]], [[Mercury (mythology)]], etc. There must then be a way to direct the reader to the correct specific article when the ambiguous word "Mercury" is referenced by linking, browsing or searching; this is what is known as ''disambiguation''. In this case, it is achieved using Mercury as the title of a ''disambiguation page''.
There are three common disambiguation scenarios, of which the following are examples: * The page at [[:en:Joker|Joker]] is a ''[[:en:Disambiguation page|Disambiguation page]]'', leading to all the alternative uses of "Joker". It has a {{tl|Disambiguation}} template at the bottom. * The page at [[Rice]] is about one usage, called the ''[[Wikipedia:Primary topic|primary topic]]'' or ''[[Wikipedia:Broad-concept article|broad-concept]]'', and there is a short message at the top of the article, called a ''[[Wikipedia:Hatnote|hatnote]]'' and created with at [[Wikipedia:Hatnote#Hatnote templates|Hatnote templates]], guiding readers to [[Rice (disambiguation)|Rice (disambiguation''')''']] for the other less common uses of the term. * The page at [[:en:Anita Hill|Anita Hill]] is about the primary topic, and there are only two other uses. The other uses are linked directly using the hatnotes created with the {{tl|For}} template; no disambiguation page is needed.
Occasionally, a link may direct a reader to a disambiguation page instead of the intended article. The original page can be edited to link directly to the correct destination. Such edits are encouraged, as [[Wikipedia:What is Wikipedia|Wikipedia is a collaborative platform]] that welcomes contributions from all users.
Occasionally, a reader may follow a link that ends up at a disambiguation page rather than at one of the links shown on that page. The original page can be edited to link directly to its intended destination. Feel free to make such edits; everyone is welcome to improve Wikipedia, including you. It could be you who makes the overall experience of Wikipedia that much better.

{{for|more comprehensive information and a larger scope|Wikipedia:Disambiguation}} {{pp-protected|small=yes}} {{Information page|H:DIS|HELP:DAB}} {{Reader help}} {{Dabnav}} '''Disambiguation''' pages on Wikipedia are used as a process of resolving conflicts in [[Wikipedia:Article titles|article titles]] that occur when a single term can be associated with more than one topic, making that term likely to be the natural title for more than one article. In other words, disambiguations are paths leading to different articles which could, in principle, have the same title.
For example, the word "Mercury" can refer to several things, including an element, a planet, and a Roman god. Since only one Wikipedia page can have the generic name [[:en:Mercury|Mercury]], ''unambiguous'' article titles are used for each of these topics: [[:en:Mercury (element)|Mercury (element)]], [[Mercury (planet)]], [[:en:Mercury (mythology)|Mercury (mythology)]], etc. There must then be a way to direct the reader to the correct specific article when the ambiguous word "Mercury" is referenced by linking, browsing or searching; this is what is known as ''disambiguation''. In this case, it is achieved using Mercury as the title of a ''disambiguation page''.
There are three common disambiguation scenarios, of which the following are examples: * The page at [[:en:Joker|Joker]] is a ''[[:en:Disambiguation page|Disambiguation page]]'', leading to all the alternative uses of "Joker". It has a {{tl|Disambiguation}} template at the bottom. * The page at [[Rice]] is about one usage, called the ''[[Wikipedia:Primary topic|primary topic]]'' or ''[[Wikipedia:Broad-concept article|broad-concept]]'', and there is a short message at the top of the article, called a ''[[Wikipedia:Hatnote|hatnote]]'' and created with at [[Wikipedia:Hatnote#Hatnote templates|Hatnote templates]], guiding readers to [[Rice (disambiguation)|Rice (disambiguation''')''']] for the other less common uses of the term. * The page at [[:en:Anita Hill|Anita Hill]] is about the primary topic, and there are only two other uses. The other uses are linked directly using the hatnotes created with the {{tl|For}} template; no disambiguation page is needed.
Occasionally, a link may direct a reader to a disambiguation page instead of the intended article. The original page can be edited to link directly to the correct destination. Such edits are encouraged, as [[Wikipedia:What is Wikipedia|Wikipedia is a collaborative platform]] that welcomes contributions from all users.
Occasionally, a reader may follow a link that ends up at a disambiguation page rather than at one of the links shown on that page. The original page can be edited to link directly to its intended destination. Feel free to make such edits; everyone is welcome to improve Wikipedia, including you. It could be you who makes the overall experience of Wikipedia that much better.

revision.text:
{{for|more comprehensive information and a larger scope|Wikipedia:Disambiguation}} {{pp-protected|small=yes}} {{Information page|H:DIS|HELP:DAB}} {{Reader help}} {{Dabnav}} '''Disambiguation''' pages on Wikipedia are used as a process of resolving conflicts in [[Wikipedia:Article titles|article titles]] that occur when a single term can be associated with more than one topic, making that term likely to be the natural title for more than one article. In other words, disambiguations are paths leading to different articles which could, in principle, have the same title.
For example, the word "Mercury" can refer to several things, including an element, a planet, and a Roman god. Since only one Wikipedia page can have the generic name [[:en:Mercury|Mercury]], ''unambiguous'' article titles are used for each of these topics: [[:en:Mercury (element)|Mercury (element)]], [[:en:Mercury (planet)|Mercury (planet)]], [[:en:Mercury (mythology)|Mercury (mythology)]], etc. There must then be a way to direct the reader to the correct specific article when the ambiguous word "Mercury" is referenced by linking, browsing or searching; this is what is known as ''disambiguation''. In this case, it is achieved using Mercury as the title of a ''disambiguation page''.
There are three common disambiguation scenarios, of which the following are examples: * The page at [[:en:Joker|Joker]] is a ''[[:en:Disambiguation page|Disambiguation page]]'', leading to all the alternative uses of "Joker". It has a {{tl|Disambiguation}} template at the bottom. * The page at [[Rice]] is about one usage, called the ''[[Wikipedia:Primary topic|primary topic]]'' or ''[[Wikipedia:Broad-concept article|broad-concept]]'', and there is a short message at the top of the article, called a ''[[Wikipedia:Hatnote|hatnote]]'' and created with at [[Wikipedia:Hatnote#Hatnote templates|Hatnote templates]], guiding readers to [[Rice (disambiguation)|Rice (disambiguation''')''']] for the other less common uses of the term. * The page at [[:en:Anita Hill|Anita Hill]] is about the primary topic, and there are only two other uses. The other uses are linked directly using the hatnotes created with the {{tl|For}} template; no disambiguation page is needed.
Occasionally, a link may direct a reader to a disambiguation page instead of the intended article. The original page can be edited to link directly to the correct destination. Such edits are encouraged, as [[Wikipedia:What is Wikipedia|Wikipedia is a collaborative platform]] that welcomes contributions from all users.

{{for|more comprehensive information and a larger scope|Wikipedia:Disambiguation}} {{pp-protected|small=yes}} {{Information page|H:DIS|HELP:DAB}} {{Reader help}} {{Dabnav}} '''Disambiguation''' pages on Wikipedia are used as a process of resolving conflicts in [[Wikipedia:Article titles|article titles]] that occur when a single term can be associated with more than one topic, making that term likely to be the natural title for more than one article. In other words, disambiguations are paths leading to different articles which could, in principle, have the same title.
For example, the word "Mercury" can refer to several things, including an element, a planet, and a Roman god. Since only one Wikipedia page can have the generic name [[:en:Mercury|Mercury]], ''unambiguous'' article titles are used for each of these topics: [[:en:Mercury (element)|Mercury (element)]], [[:en:Mercury (planet)|Mercury (planet)]], [[:en:Mercury (mythology)|Mercury (mythology)]], etc. There must then be a way to direct the reader to the correct specific article when the ambiguous word "Mercury" is referenced by linking, browsing or searching; this is what is known as ''disambiguation''. In this case, it is achieved using Mercury as the title of a ''disambiguation page''.
There are three common disambiguation scenarios, of which the following are examples: * The page at [[:en:Joker|Joker]] is a ''[[:en:Disambiguation page|Disambiguation page]]'', leading to all the alternative uses of "Joker". It has a {{tl|Disambiguation}} template at the bottom. * The page at [[:en:Rice|Rice]] is about one usage, called the ''[[Wikipedia:Primary topic|primary topic]]'' or ''[[Wikipedia:Broad-concept article|broad-concept]]'', and there is a short message at the top of the article, called a ''[[Wikipedia:Hatnote|hatnote]]'' and created with at [[Wikipedia:Hatnote#Hatnote templates|Hatnote templates]], guiding readers to [[Rice (disambiguation)|Rice (disambiguation''')''']] for the other less common uses of the term. * The page at [[:en:Anita Hill|Anita Hill]] is about the primary topic, and there are only two other uses. The other uses are linked directly using the hatnotes created with the {{tl|For}} template; no disambiguation page is needed.

{{for|more comprehensive information and a larger scope|Wikipedia:Disambiguation}} {{pp-protected|small=yes}} {{Information page|H:DIS|HELP:DAB}} {{Reader help}} {{Dabnav}} '''Disambiguation''' pages on Wikipedia are used as a process of resolving conflicts in [[Wikipedia:Article titles|article titles]] that occur when a single term can be associated with more than one topic, making that term likely to be the natural title for more than one article. In other words, disambiguations are paths leading to different articles which could, in principle, have the same title.
For example, the word "Mercury" can refer to several things, including an element, a planet, and a Roman god. Since only one Wikipedia page can have the generic name [[:en:Mercury|Mercury]], ''unambiguous'' article titles are used for each of these topics: [[:en:Mercury (element)|Mercury (element)]], [[:en:Mercury (planet)|Mercury (planet)]], [[:en:Mercury (mythology)|Mercury (mythology)]], etc. There must then be a way to direct the reader to the correct specific article when the ambiguous word "Mercury" is referenced by linking, browsing or searching; this is what is known as ''disambiguation''. In this case, it is achieved using Mercury as the title of a ''disambiguation page''.
There are three common disambiguation scenarios, of which the following are examples: * The page at [[:en:Joker|Joker]] is a ''[[:en:Disambiguation page|Disambiguation page]]'', leading to all the alternative uses of "Joker". It has a {{tl|Disambiguation}} template at the bottom. * The page at [[:en:Rice|Rice]] is about one usage, called the ''[[Wikipedia:Primary topic|primary topic]]'' or ''[[Wikipedia:Broad-concept article|broad-concept]]'', and there is a short message at the top of the article, called a ''[[Wikipedia:Hatnote|hatnote]]'' and created with at [[Wikipedia:Hatnote#Hatnote templates|Hatnote templates]], guiding readers to [[:en:Rice (disambiguation)|Rice (disambiguation''')''']] for the other less common uses of the term. * The page at [[:en:Anita Hill|Anita Hill]] is about the primary topic, and there are only two other uses. revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was 1 revision imported
revision.text:
{{for|more comprehensive information and a larger scope|Wikipedia:Disambiguation}} {{pp-protected|small=yes}} {{Information page|H:DIS|HELP:DAB}} {{Reader help}} {{Dabnav}} '''Disambiguation''' pages on Wikipedia are used as a process of resolving conflicts in [[Wikipedia:Article titles|article titles]] that occur when a single term can be associated with more than one topic, making that term likely to be the natural title for more than one article. In other words, disambiguations are paths leading to different articles which could, in principle, have the same title.
For example, the word "Mercury" can refer to several things, including an element, a planet, and a Roman god. Since only one Wikipedia page can have the generic name [[:en:Mercury|Mercury]], ''unambiguous'' article titles are used for each of these topics: [[:en:Mercury (element)|Mercury (element)]], [[:en:Mercury (planet)|Mercury (planet)]], [[:en:Mercury (mythology)|Mercury (mythology)]], etc. There must then be a way to direct the reader to the correct specific article when the ambiguous word "Mercury" is referenced by linking, browsing or searching; this is what is known as ''disambiguation''. In this case, it is achieved using Mercury as the title of a ''disambiguation page''.
There are three common disambiguation scenarios, of which the following are examples: * The page at [[:en:Joker|Joker]] is a ''[[:en:Disambiguation page|Disambiguation page]]'', leading to all the alternative uses of "Joker". It has a {{tl|Disambiguation}} template at the bottom. * The page at [[:en:Rice|Rice]] is about one usage, called the ''[[Wikipedia:Primary topic|primary topic]]'' or ''[[Wikipedia:Broad-concept article|broad-concept]]'', and there is a short message at the top of the article, called a ''[[Wikipedia:Hatnote|hatnote]]'' and created with at [[Wikipedia:Hatnote#Hatnote templates|Hatnote templates]], guiding readers to [[:en:Rice (disambiguation)|Rice (disambiguation''')''']] for the other less common uses of the term. * The page at [[:en:Anita Hill|Anita Hill]] is about the primary topic, and there are only two other uses. The other uses are linked directly using the hatnotes created with the {{tl|For}} template; no disambiguation page is needed.
Occasionally, a link may direct a reader to a disambiguation page instead of the intended article. The original page can be edited to link directly to the correct destination. Such edits are encouraged, as [[Wikipedia:What is Wikipedia|Wikipedia is a collaborative platform]] that welcomes contributions from all users.
Occasionally, a reader may follow a link that ends up at a disambiguation page rather than at one of the links shown on that page. The original page can be edited to link directly to its intended destination. Feel free to make such edits; everyone is welcome to improve Wikipedia, including you. It could be you who makes the overall experience of Wikipedia that much better.
== See also ==
* [[Help:Contents]] * [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Disambiguation pages]] * [[Wikipedia:Template messages for disambiguation and redirection]] * [[Wikipedia:Disambiguation detector]] * the {{tl|Distinguish}} template in [[Wikipedia:Hatnote]]
{{-}} ---- <inputbox> type=search width=30 namespaces=Main**,Portal**,Category**,Help**, searchbuttonlabel=Search break=no </inputbox> ----
[[Category:Reader help]] [[Category:Wikipedia disambiguation| ]] [[sn:Disambiguation]] [[sn:Disambiguation]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[sn:" to "[[Category:sn_crud|TEST"
revision.text:
{{for|more comprehensive information and a larger scope|Wikipedia:Disambiguation}} {{pp-protected|small=yes}} {{Information page|H:DIS|HELP:DAB}} {{Reader help}} {{Dabnav}} '''Disambiguation''' pages on Wikipedia are used as a process of resolving conflicts in [[Wikipedia:Article titles|article titles]] that occur when a single term can be associated with more than one topic, making that term likely to be the natural title for more than one article. In other words, disambiguations are paths leading to different articles which could, in principle, have the same title.
For example, the word "Mercury" can refer to several things, including an element, a planet, and a Roman god. Since only one Wikipedia page can have the generic name [[:en:Mercury|Mercury]], ''unambiguous'' article titles are used for each of these topics: [[:en:Mercury (element)|Mercury (element)]], [[:en:Mercury (planet)|Mercury (planet)]], [[:en:Mercury (mythology)|Mercury (mythology)]], etc. There must then be a way to direct the reader to the correct specific article when the ambiguous word "Mercury" is referenced by linking, browsing or searching; this is what is known as ''disambiguation''. In this case, it is achieved using Mercury as the title of a ''disambiguation page''.
There are three common disambiguation scenarios, of which the following are examples: * The page at [[:en:Joker|Joker]] is a ''[[:en:Disambiguation page|Disambiguation page]]'', leading to all the alternative uses of "Joker". It has a {{tl|Disambiguation}} template at the bottom. * The page at [[:en:Rice|Rice]] is about one usage, called the ''[[Wikipedia:Primary topic|primary topic]]'' or ''[[Wikipedia:Broad-concept article|broad-concept]]'', and there is a short message at the top of the article, called a ''[[Wikipedia:Hatnote|hatnote]]'' and created with at [[Wikipedia:Hatnote#Hatnote templates|Hatnote templates]], guiding readers to [[:en:Rice (disambiguation)|Rice (disambiguation''')''']] for the other less common uses of the term. * The page at [[:en:Anita Hill|Anita Hill]] is about the primary topic, and there are only two other uses. The other uses are linked directly using the hatnotes created with the {{tl|For}} template; no disambiguation page is needed.
Occasionally, a link may direct a reader to a disambiguation page instead of the intended article. The original page can be edited to link directly to the correct destination. Such edits are encouraged, as [[Wikipedia:What is Wikipedia|Wikipedia is a collaborative platform]] that welcomes contributions from all users.
Occasionally, a reader may follow a link that ends up at a disambiguation page rather than at one of the links shown on that page. The original page can be edited to link directly to its intended destination. Feel free to make such edits; everyone is welcome to improve Wikipedia, including you. It could be you who makes the overall experience of Wikipedia that much better.
== See also ==
* [[Help:Contents]] * [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Disambiguation pages]] * [[Wikipedia:Template messages for disambiguation and redirection]] * [[Wikipedia:Disambiguation detector]] * the {{tl|Distinguish}} template in [[Wikipedia:Hatnote]]
{{-}} ---- <inputbox> type=search width=30 namespaces=Main**,Portal**,Category**,Help**, searchbuttonlabel=Search break=no </inputbox> ----
[[Category:Reader help]] [[Category:Wikipedia disambiguation| ]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTDisambiguation]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTDisambiguation]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24221752933|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{for|more comprehensive information and a larger scope|Wikipedia:Disambiguation}} {{pp-protected|small=yes}} {{Information page|H:DIS|HELP:DAB}} {{Reader help}} {{Dabnav}} '''Disambiguation''' pages on Wikipedia are used as a process of resolving conflicts in [[Wikipedia:Article titles|article titles]] that occur when a single term can be associated with more than one topic, making that term likely to be the natural title for more than one article. In other words, disambiguations are paths leading to different articles which could, in principle, have the same title.
For example, the word "Mercury" can refer to several things, including an element, a planet, and a Roman god. Since only one Wikipedia page can have the generic name [[:en:Mercury|Mercury]], ''unambiguous'' article titles are used for each of these topics: [[:en:Mercury (element)|Mercury (element)]], [[:en:Mercury (planet)|Mercury (planet)]], [[:en:Mercury (mythology)|Mercury (mythology)]], etc. There must then be a way to direct the reader to the correct specific article when the ambiguous word "Mercury" is referenced by linking, browsing or searching; this is what is known as ''disambiguation''. In this case, it is achieved using Mercury as the title of a ''disambiguation page''.
There are three common disambiguation scenarios, of which the following are examples: * The page at [[:en:Joker|Joker]] is a ''[[:en:Disambiguation page|Disambiguation page]]'', leading to all the alternative uses of "Joker". It has a {{tl|Disambiguation}} template at the bottom. * The page at [[:en:Rice|Rice]] is about one usage, called the ''[[Wikipedia:Primary topic|primary topic]]'' or ''[[Wikipedia:Broad-concept article|broad-concept]]'', and there is a short message at the top of the article, called a ''[[Wikipedia:Hatnote|hatnote]]'' and created with at [[Wikipedia:Hatnote#Hatnote templates|Hatnote templates]], guiding readers to [[:en:Rice (disambiguation)|Rice (disambiguation''')''']] for the other less common uses of the term. * The page at [[:en:Anita Hill|Anita Hill]] is about the primary topic, and there are only two other uses. The other uses are linked directly using the hatnotes created with the {{tl|For}} template; no disambiguation page is needed.
Occasionally, a link may direct a reader to a disambiguation page instead of the intended article. The original page can be edited to link directly to the correct destination. Such edits are encouraged, as [[Wikipedia:What is Wikipedia|Wikipedia is a collaborative platform]] that welcomes contributions from all users.
Occasionally, a reader may follow a link that ends up at a disambiguation page rather than at one of the links shown on that page. The original page can be edited to link directly to its intended destination. Feel free to make such edits; everyone is welcome to improve Wikipedia, including you. It could be you who makes the overall experience of Wikipedia that much better.
== See also ==
